title: Log event `data` for `type`: trust.attest
summary: Records publication or update of a trust attestation (see spec §6). Verifiers use the attestation object id and `trust.revoke` for lifecycle.

attestation_id.description: The `id` of the Trust Attestation object (e.g. `relay:attestation:...`) this event attests to.

target_rules.description: `target` on the log event SHOULD reference the same attestation or the subject of the attestation, per implementation profile. If the spec is silent, `target` MAY be the `attestation_id` for deduplication. Registry consumers SHOULD document chosen binding.

validation_rules: `attestation_id` MUST be a non-empty string in the deployment's attestation id format.
Origin SHOULD reject the append if the object at `attestation_id` is not a valid signed Trust Attestation object verifiable at insert time (§6).
`data` values MUST be canonical per §4 when hashed or signed with the log envelope.

compatibility.forward: Add optional keys in `data`: readers MUST ignore unknown keys for the same `registry` file `document_version` family unless `ext_required` on a wrapper object says otherwise (core §22.4 / §22.5).
compatibility.backward: If `attestation_id` is missing, event is **invalid** for v1.0+ interop for this type.
compatibility.version_bump: Breaking changes to required keys or types require a new file version (e.g. `v2`).
